title: Name is specified as an argument.

comments.body:
I find the handling of --root and --link to be confusing here?  When would I specify these?  
Wouldn't I just use

buildah mount ID /mnt

And then be able to put my content into /mnt?

The implementation of some of the storage drivers requires that only the drivers themselves decide where a layer gets mounted, so in the example above, `/mnt` could never be the layer's main mount point.  Adding a symbolic link from a specified location that points to the root directory of a container's read-write layer is an affordance, but given that we return the location when `mount` succeeds, I wouldn't mind if we stopped.  Alternately, we could rip symlinks out and replace them with bind mounts.

I am looking for the most obvious command to the user which I think is 

buildah mount SRC DEST
@nalind This now passes tests and requires that the name/id is always passed.
